{{Quote|You want to see what I have in stock? I know my kiosk isn't much to look at, but my prices are reasonable and the merchandise is sound.|translated=1|Larrim, advertising their store|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
'''Larrim''' was a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. They ran a [[Larrim's kiosk|kiosk]] in the [[South Apartments]] of the [[Upper City]], where they sold various goods to residents and passers-by. In [[3956 BBY]], following the [[Attack on the Endar Spire|attack]] on the Republic [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''[[Endar Spire]]'' by a [[Sith armada (Jedi Civil War)|Sith fleet]], Larrim approached a survivor of the attack and former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]], [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who had the memories of a [[Republic soldier]]. Larrim explained who they were and advertised the items they had for sale to Revan.

==Biography==
===Running a kiosk===
{{Quote|I run a little shop here in this old building, selling basic equipment to the illegal aliens renting out these apartments.|translated=1|Larrim, to Revan|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
[[File:LarrimKiosk.png|thumb|left|325px|Larrim ran a kiosk that sold mostly to non-humans in Taris's Upper City.]]
Larrim lived on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. The [[Human/Legends|Human]] residents of the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] were [[Speciesism|speciesist]], and it was illegal for non-Humans to live in the [[Upper City]]. Despite this, several [[Alien/Legends|non-humans]] lived in the Upper City's [[South Apartments]], as they were the only ones who would rent in such a run-down location. The Tarisian authorities found it to be too much hassle to enforce the law, and did not persecute the aliens living there. Larrim operated a small [[Larrim's kiosk|kiosk]] in the apartments, where they sold supplies to the local residents, including a rare, expensive, and hi-tech [[Personal energy shield/Legends|personal energy shield]].<ref name="KotOR" />

In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' in [[3956 BBY]].</ref> a [[Attack on the Endar Spire|battle]] between the Republic [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''[[Endar Spire]]'' and a [[Sith armada (Jedi Civil War)|Sith fleet]] raged [[Orbit/Legends|above]] the planet. The battle resulted in the destruction of the ''Endar Spire'', with several [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] from the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] crashing on Taris's surface. One of these pods contained the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Bastila Shan]], whose [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] [[Force power/Legends|skill]] made her a valuable part of the Republic's war effort. In an attempt to capture Shan after the battle overhead, [[Darth Malak]], the incumbent [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], ordered a planet-wide occupation and quarantine, preventing anyone from landing on or leaving the [[City/Legends|city]] world, and hurting many businesses operating on the planet. Despite this, the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] authorities also considered the non-humans living illegally in the Upper City to not be worth the trouble, and they too did not enforce Taris's speciesist laws.<ref name="KotOR" />

===Meeting Revan===
{{Quote|Well, I don't see to many of your kind around here. Most of the residents in this run down old apartment are illegal aliens. My name is Larrim, by the way.|translated=1|Larrim, to Revan|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
[[File:Kadir11.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Larrim directed Revan's questions to Kadir (pictured), the janitor of the building.]]
Former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], whose memory had been [[Amnesia/Legends|destroyed]] and replaced with those of a [[Republic soldier]], was aboard the ''Endar Spire'' during the battle and boarded an escape pod with Republic war hero [[Carth Onasi]]. The pod landed in Taris's Upper City, with the crash knocking Revan unconscious. Onasi dragged Revan to the South Apartments, where the two hid in an abandoned apartment for several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] before Revan fully regained consciousness. When Revan woke up, the pair decided to [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|search]] for Shan before the Sith found her.<ref name="KotOR" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
Larrim, at their kiosk in the South Apartments, approached Revan when<ref name="KotOR" /> he<ref name="Campaign guide">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> walked by. Larrim introduced themself to Revan and told him about the energy [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] they had in stock. Revan asked about the shield, and Larrim explained that it protected from energy attacks, including [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], but not from [[Ion blaster/Legends|ion blasters]]. They also told Revan about the rumors of the shield being derived from an [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] design. Revan asked Larrim about the kiosk and sought information about the plight of the non-humans living illegally in the Upper City. Larrim gladly described their business and explained the [[Humanocentrism|humanocentric]] laws of Taris. They were, however, reluctant to answer too many questions unrelated to business for fear of losing the trust of potential buyers. They instead directed Revan to speak to the [[Janitor/Legends|janitor]], [[Kadir (Tarisian)|Kadir]], whom they described as "bordering on senile."<ref name="KotOR" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Questions? I'd rather not. It might be bad for business if people saw me answering a bunch of questions for a human. Most of the residents here are illegal aliens, questions are frowned on. I don't want to lose the trust of my customers.|translated=1|Larrim, to Revan|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
Larrim was a [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned and gray-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]]<ref name="KotOR" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]].<ref name="Gender" /> They wore their [[Lekku/Legends|lekku]] wrapped around their neck. Larrim feared losing the trust of their customers and avoided answering questions unrelated to themself or their business. Despite this, they were willing to both buy from and sell to Revan, who was a new customer. Larrim was unafraid of being caught by Tarisian authorities or the Sith occupiers, knowing they had long ago turned a blind eye to the South Apartments. In addition to speaking the [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]] [[Language/Legends|language]], Larrim was able to understand spoken [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="KotOR" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|I know it's really none of my business, but you look like someone who might need to purchase one of those new energy shields. They're the latest thing, you know. Very high tech.|translated=1|Larrim, to Revan|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
Larrim wore a green [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]] with yellow accents and white pants. They also wore a brown and white [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]] and [[Boot/Legends|boots]] that were white, yellow, and brown. Larrim owned a large amount of equipment that they sold to the residents of the South Apartments. Their supplies included [[Medpac/Legends|medpacs]], [[Computer spike/Legends|computer spikes]], [[Droid/Legends|droid]] repair parts, [[Armor/Legends|armor]] suits, [[Stealth field generator/Legends|stealth field generators]], and [[Battle stimulant/Legends|battle stimulants]]. They also owned a surplus of [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]], which included [[Sword/Legends|swords]], [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]], [[Stun baton/Legends|stun batons]], [[Quarterstaff/Legends|quarterstaffs]], and a variety of blasters, [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]], and [[Mine/Legends|mines]]. In addition, they owned a single, expensive energy shield that they attempted to sell to passers-by.<ref name="KotOR" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|And I can tell you from experience that they aren't any good at all against a simple vibroblade.|Carth, in optional dialogue|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
Larrim appeared as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. If the player walks by Larrim's kiosk, they will speak to Revan in an unavoidable encounter. The player may select several dialogue choices, but the conversation does not progress any plot or quest, nor does it change the player's alignment. If Carth Onasi is in the player's party when they encounter Larrim, he will comment about the personal energy shields being useless against vibroblades, causing the Republic to train their [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] in melee combat. If the player speaks to Larrim while dressed in [[Sith trooper armor/Legends|Sith armor]], they will wish Revan a good day and ensure they do not want any trouble with the Sith.<ref name="KotOR" />
